headlines.title: Thames Water closer to nationalisation after government objects to rescue deal
headlines.description: The environment secretary says the deal does not do enough for consumers or the environment.

headlines.title: Starmer pleased 'justice has been done' after arson attacks on property connected to him
headlines.description: The prime minister said he was "very pleased for my family's sake" that two men had been convicted of the 2025 arson attacks.

headlines.title: Woman left traumatised by swinging says website 'facilitated abuse'
headlines.description: She wants to warn others after her husband pressured her into sex she did not want, she says.

headlines.title: Prince George to attend Eton College from September
headlines.description: He will follow in the footsteps of his father, the Prince of Wales, who also attended the private school.

headlines.title: Five big questions about the UK's under-16s social media ban
headlines.description: A ban is coming - but it's still not clear what it will mean for sites including Roblox, YouTube and WhatsApp.

headlines.title: Williams sisters will play Wimbledon doubles after receiving wildcard
headlines.description: Serena Williams is given a wildcard to play alongside older sister Venus in the Wimbledon women's doubles.

headlines.title: Married at First Sight Australia allegations 'disturbing', says country's watchdog
headlines.description: The claims also prompted a response from UK media regulator Ofcom, who called them "deeply concerning".

headlines.title: 'Little hero', 12, rescued drowning friend from the sea by her hair
headlines.description: Tamika and Sofia-Ann were cut off by the tide and pulled under water by the waves at Abergele.

headlines.title: Watch: California wildfires rage near passing vehicles
headlines.description: The fires in Riverside County cover over 2,000 acres, say authorities.

headlines.title: Russian artist and Putin critic shot dead in Poland
headlines.description: Robert Kuzovkov, who used the pseudonym Semyon Skrepetsky, has been known for his caricatures of politicians including Vladimir Putin.

headlines.title: England's Tino Livramento could miss World Cup after suffering fresh injury
headlines.description: England defender Tino Livramento could miss England's World Cup campaign after suffering a fresh injury on the eve of the Three Lions' tournament opener.

headlines.title: Iran 'most oppressed' team in tournament, head coach says
headlines.description: Head coach Amir Ghalenoei says his Iran side are the "most oppressed in the World Cup" after having last-minute travel restrictions imposed on them.

headlines.title: 'Daylight robbery but worth it' - what fans are spending
headlines.description: Fans in the US, Canada and Mexico are spending big on tickets, transport and accommodation - is it worth it?

headlines.title: Tunisia sack head coach just one game into World Cup
headlines.description: Tunisia sack Sabri Lamouchi one game into their World Cup, with former Morocco and Saudi Arabia manager Herve Renard replacing him.

headlines.title: What one country's experiment says about attempts to boost birth rates
headlines.description: Why did Hungary’s pronatalist approach deliver an early rise in births only then to fall back? And what lessons does it offer to other countries desperate to lift fertility?

headlines.title: Iran deal presents political nightmare for Netanyahu
headlines.description: Donald Trump's ceasefire agreement with Iran leaves the Israeli PM trapped in a new political and security dilemma.

headlines.title: When will social media ban start, and which apps will be affected?
headlines.description: The measures will see apps including TikTok and Snapchat banned for UK teens early in 2027.

headlines.title: Recovery of Ebola patients offers rare moments of joy at epicentre of outbreak
headlines.description: There are glimpses of happiness in the Democratic Republic of Congo's fight against the virus that has killed more than 170.

headlines.title: No, there’s no plot to make you allergic to meat
headlines.description: Viral conspiracy posts claim there is a secret effort to spread ticks on US farms to trigger an allergy to red meat.

headlines.title: Man bailed after woman pushed into path of bus on Putney Bridge
headlines.description: A man is bailed after being arrested in the case of a woman who was pushed into the road in 2017.

headlines.title: Serious concerns about maternity unit raised in NHS report days before landmark baby death case
headlines.description: A previously unpublished report details problems in Nottingham's maternity units before a major review.

headlines.title: Woman in court over fatal Wimbledon school crash
headlines.description: Claire Freemantle was driving the car that killed Nuria Sajjad and Selena Lau outside a school in 2023.

headlines.title: Donaldson sex abuse trial jury asked to recall visible 'pain and hurt' of accusers
headlines.description: Sir Jeffrey Donaldson, the former Democratic Unionist Party leader, is on trial for 18 sex abuse charges, including one count of rape, all of which he denies.

headlines.title: Newsreader Tina Daheley to leave Radio 2 breakfast show
headlines.description: The move comes just a couple of weeks before Sara Cox takes over the show in July.

headlines.title: Eight dead after US Air Force B-52 bomber crashes in California
headlines.description: The incident occurred on Monday morning while the aircraft had been on a routine test mission.

headlines.title: Trust in news hits a new low, research suggests
headlines.description: The Reuters Institute records its lowest trust figures since the annual research began in 2015.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Social Media Ban For Under-16s
headlines.description: Under-16s to be banned from social media Keir Starmer announces.

headlines.title: Americanswers… on 5 Live! Is Trump still fit for the job on his 80th birthday?
headlines.description: Donald Trump is now the second oldest president in US history

headlines.title: Livramento's World Cup in doubt as Chalobah poised to step in
headlines.description: England defender Tino Livramento could miss England's World Cup campaign after suffering a fresh injury on the eve of the Three Lions' tournament opener.

headlines.title: Tuchel's England deal includes performance clause
headlines.description: Thomas Tuchel's England contract extension is subject to a performance clause, says FA chief executive Mark Bullingham.

headlines.title: Williams sisters to play together at Wimbledon
headlines.description: Serena Williams is given a wildcard to play alongside older sister Venus in the Wimbledon women's doubles.

headlines.title: Meet the Iraq player set to make history for Pakistan
headlines.description: Zidane Iqbal, a former Manchester United player, will make history when he plays for Iraq this summer, becoming the first player of Pakistani heritage to feature at a men's World Cup.

headlines.title: What does the future hold for Rashford?
headlines.description: Barcelona decided not to trigger the option that would have allowed them to sign Marcus Rashford for £26m, so what does the future hold for the England World Cup star?

headlines.title: Tunisia sack Lamouchi just one game into World Cup
headlines.description: Tunisia sack Sabri Lamouchi one game into their World Cup, with former Morocco and Saudi Arabia manager Herve Renard replacing him.
